title: HEAT Letter: Zero-parameter redshift evolution of late-type galaxy sizes from a0(z) proportional to H(z)
description: Companion code and LaTeX source for the HEAT (Hubble-Emergent Acceleration Theory) letter. HEAT identifies the MOND acceleration scale with the FRW apparent-horizon surface gravity divided by 2 pi, a0(z) = c H(z) / (2 pi), and predicts the late-type size-redshift relation R(z)/R_0 = (1+z)^{-3/4} [Om_m(z)/Om_m(0)]^{1/4} at fixed baryonic mass. The headline observable is invariant under the proportionality constant: only the scaling a0 proportional to H(z) enters, while the 1/(2 pi) is anchored to SPARC at z=0. This release reproduces the six figures of the letter: (i) the closed-form R(z)/R_0 normalisation curve with its asymptote Om_m^{-1/4} and the MMW98 disk-formation baseline as reference; (ii) the stellar-mass-normalised compactification R_obs/R_0 versus redshift; (iii) the ALMA z ~ 4.5 joint kinematic test (N=4); (iv) the BTFR zero-point evolution from SPARC + cosmic-noon + ALMA; (v) the SPARC 171-galaxy local-anchor chi^2 comparison with constant-a0 MOND (p=0.46); and (vi) the quantitative mass-selection robustness test ruling out one-parameter M_b(z) mimics of R(z). The hypothesis stands or falls on percent-level R(z)/R_0 over 0 lesssim z lesssim 3 from Euclid/Roman. Included are the analysis scripts, the Rotmod_LTG SPARC rotation-curve catalogue, the paper LaTeX source (publication/paper_heat_letter.tex), and the pre-generated figures under heat_output/.
